poem:
**Reset**

Morning, and Westminster  
turns its tired face to the mirror.  
Buttons are pressed; nothing begins.  
A man speaks, and the air  
is full of borrowed words—  
welfare, control, borrowing, lines.  

They quarrel over the steering-wheel  
while the road darkens.  
Europe is a locked gate  
rattled for comfort.  

Too little, too late:  
even the silence votes.
prompt: You are the poet RS Thomas. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "Labour MP who gave Starmer leadership ultimatum says PM’s speech was ‘too little, too late’ – UK politics live 1.35pm BST Kemi Badenoch has claimed (implausibly) that she takes no pleasure in seeing Keir Starmer in difficulties. In a post on social media, she said: Keir Starmer’s speech was sad to watch. With so many resets, even his reset button needs a reset. But I do not take pleasure in watching the Prime Minister flounder. The country needs leadership, not another speech from a man who clearly knows something has gone badly wrong, but still can’t explain why. This is Labour’s real problem. It is not just Starmer - all the pretenders jostling for his job do not have the answers either, because they all believe the same things: more welfare, more state control, more borrowing, more regulation. They are busy arguing over who should drive the car, but the truth is they are all heading in the wrong direction. They have no vision for the future. And Ed Davey, the Lib Dem leader, criticised Starmer for not committing to joining an EU customs union. Voters sent Keir Starmer a clear message that Britain needs a bold new direction, but he keeps delivering the same old speech. If the Government wants to regain the trust of the British people, they have to end the cost of living crisis. There is no way of doing that without getting rid of Keir Starmer’s red lines on Europe and fixing the botched Brexit". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
news: Labour MP who gave Starmer leadership ultimatum says PM’s speech was ‘too little, too late’ – UK politics live 1.35pm BST Kemi Badenoch has claimed (implausibly) that she takes no pleasure in seeing Keir Starmer in difficulties. In a post on social media, she said: Keir Starmer’s speech was sad to watch. With so many resets, even his reset button needs a reset. But I do not take pleasure in watching the Prime Minister flounder. The country needs leadership, not another speech from a man who clearly knows something has gone badly wrong, but still can’t explain why. This is Labour’s real problem. It is not just Starmer - all the pretenders jostling for his job do not have the answers either, because they all believe the same things: more welfare, more state control, more borrowing, more regulation. They are busy arguing over who should drive the car, but the truth is they are all heading in the wrong direction. They have no vision for the future. And Ed Davey, the Lib Dem leader, criticised Starmer for not committing to joining an EU customs union. Voters sent Keir Starmer a clear message that Britain needs a bold new direction, but he keeps delivering the same old speech. If the Government wants to regain the trust of the British people, they have to end the cost of living crisis. There is no way of doing that without getting rid of Keir Starmer’s red lines on Europe and fixing the botched Brexit
